scaled_oracle_cases.description: Baseline case: single tumor input with valid data, ensuring the function still parses correctly under the new validation constraint.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function retains correct behavior for single-tumor input after adding multi-tumor support and validation.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must parse single-tumor files and produce the expected dictionary with flat lists, and must pass the new validation (no mismatch).
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that only handles multi-tumor or breaks single-tumor parsing would fail.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must test single and multi-tumor inputs.
Add constraint that validates the number of tumor fractions matches the number of copy number and probability groups, raising errors on mismatch.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Multi-tumor input: verify correct parsing of two tumors with nested lists for C and p*.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly parses a file with two tumors, returning nested lists for C and p*.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must handle mu with three values (mu_normal, two tumors), C colon-separated groups each containing comma-separated copies, and p* similarly structured.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A naive parser that treats all fields as flat lists would produce incorrect output or crash.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.3,0.3\t2,4:3,5\t0.1,0.3:0.2,0.4\n"
)
fname = "theta_multi.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must test single and multi-tumor inputs.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Mismatch between number of tumors and C groups should raise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function raises ValueError when number of tumor fractions does not match number of colon-separated groups in C and p*.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must validate that len(mu_tumors) equals number of tumor copy number pairs derived from C structure.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A function without validation would parse mismatched input without error, producing incorrect output.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n12.5\t0.4,0.6,0.3\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n"
fname = "theta_bad.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include at least one case that triggers the validation error.
